title: Init Containers Behaviour
description:
## Init Containers Behaviour

A pod spec defines two init containers (`init-db-check` and `init-config`) followed by one main app container (`web`).

Which statement **correctly** describes how Kubernetes runs these containers?

options.text: All three containers start simultaneously; init containers simply have lower priority
Init containers run sequentially to completion before the main `web` container starts
The `web` container starts first, and init containers run as sidecars alongside it
Init containers cannot share volumes with the main container

explanation: Init containers always run **sequentially** and must each exit with a success (exit code 0) before the next one starts. Only after **all** init containers complete successfully does Kubernetes start the main application containers. They can share volumes with main containers, making them ideal for setup tasks like seeding configs, waiting for dependencies, or initialising databases.

hints.title: Init container execution order
hints.body: Check `kubectl explain pod.spec.initContainers`. Each init container runs to completion before the next begins, and all must succeed before app containers start.
